subject: Re: CZM Meeting
body:
In 4 I don't understand the temporary removal..issue..
what was the attitude???

--- John Amerling <jamerling@rrcaribbean.com> wrote:

> I SHOULD ADD A POSTCRIPT. IN MY MEETING WITH HODGE
> HE WELCOMED THE PROSPECT
> OF SELF INSPECTION OF CONSTRUCITON WORK. THE
> PRACTICE IS MORE EXPENSIVE, BUT
> IT WILL AVOID A LOT OF DELAY. 
> 
>  
> 
> Here is a summary of what happened today in meeting
> with Mr. Oriel and Ms
> Peterson;
> 
>  
> 
> 1.	I showed them portions of our study of our report
> concerning
> existing conditions and future plans.
> 2.	Ms Peterson suggested  that:
> 
> a.	 we take the full buildout drawing, FB 1, and
> flesh it out with
> phase designations and a brief narrative and submit
> it covered by a Minor
> Application form; 
> b.	the agency would accept this and then use it as a
> baseline document
> in connection with future reviews of all
> applications
> 
> 3.	They suggested that both the office and the pool
> related expansions
> be submitted as a modification to Permit
> #CZT-53-04L:
> 
> a.	The modification would seek approval of both the
> office and the pool
> expansions
> b.	The modification would mention infrastructure
> improvements which we
> want to cover, such as trenching for utilities and
> placement of pads and
> structures to house the R/O expansion
> c.	The modification would mention the request to add
> water to the salt
> pond on the grounds that this was a land side, not a
> marine issue
> d.	The cover letter would say that the current minor
> application for
> the office is being withdrawn; they swore up and
> down that the would review
> the modification right away and not set the whole
> process back.
> 
> 4.	Mr. Oriel continued to advocate using the base
> permit for the dock
> as the fulcrum for dealing with any changes we
> needed to the barging areas,
> stone in the area of the urchins, floating dock and
> so on; he mentioned the
> need to ask for a waiver of time or "temporary
> removal" of these items from
> the Corps stuff if this were to occur; I was
> non-committal. 
> 5.	On the issue of dock lights we took out the
> approved dock plans
> which do have electrical details; since electrical
> work of one sort or
> another has already been approved, the agency would
> be satisfied if we
> simply sent a letter saying that we are modifying
> those details to the
> extent that we are putting in an emergency light
> system, probably the
> existing letter would suffice.
> 6.	It appears that once this is done we could and
> would come in with a
> series of minor permit applications for the main
> house, library and so on.
> 7.	I also took the time to meet with Douglas Hodge
> to go over the
> building permit application process which we are
> initiating for the various
> buildings, which, as you know, will be on a separate
> track; he is primed and
> I want to get the office and pool documents into his
> hands asap. 
> 8.	We did spend some time on the R/O in take issue:
> 
> a.	When we were on the island yesterday, Mr. Oriel
> mentioned that at
> one time a new R/O intake line was requested on the
> south east side of the
> island;
> b.	Apparently that request was dropped and a line
> running out or under
> the dock was substituted;
> c.	What is the scope of the current request?
> d.	The current master plan does not contemplate a
> new intake line; a
> new well is contemplated; I would prefer figuring
> out a well strategy and
> getting away from intake approvals because we get
> away from a lot of
> bureaucracy in doing so.
> 
> 9.	There is something in me that makes me nervous
> about withdrawing the
> office permit, but I have the impression that the
> people we are dealing with
> are being honest and they will complete reviews
> promptly.
> 10.	I am going to get some work done on the master
> plan document and I
> am going to target filing it next week; I should
> like input from you both
> about the content of this message. 
> 
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
In 4 I don't understand the temporary removal..issue..
what was the attitude???

--- John Amerling jamerling@rrcaribbean.com wrote:

I SHOULD ADD A POSTCRIPT. IN MY MEETING WITH HODGE
HE WELCOMED THE PROSPECT
OF SELF INSPECTION OF CONSTRUCITON WORK. THE
PRACTICE IS MORE EXPENSIVE, BUT
IT WILL AVOID A LOT OF DELAY.

Here is a summary of what happened today in meeting
with Mr. Oriel and Ms
Peterson;

  1. I showed them portions of our study of our report
    concerning
    existing conditions and future plans.
  2. Ms Peterson suggested that:

a. we take the full buildout drawing, FB 1, and
flesh it out with
phase designations and a brief narrative and submit
it covered by a Minor
Application form;
b. the agency would accept this and then use it as a
baseline document
in connection with future reviews of all
applications

  1. They suggested that both the office and the pool
    related expansions
    be submitted as a modification to Permit

#CZT-53-04L:

a. The modification would seek approval of both the
office and the pool
expansions
b. The modification would mention infrastructure
improvements which we
want to cover, such as trenching for utilities and
placement of pads and
structures to house the R/O expansion
c. The modification would mention the request to add
water to the salt
pond on the grounds that this was a land side, not a
marine issue
d. The cover letter would say that the current minor
application for
the office is being withdrawn; they swore up and
down that the would review
the modification right away and not set the whole
process back.

  1. Mr. Oriel continued to advocate using the base
    permit for the dock
    as the fulcrum for dealing with any changes we
    needed to the barging areas,
    stone in the area of the urchins, floating dock and
    so on; he mentioned the
    need to ask for a waiver of time or "temporary
    removal" of these items from
    the Corps stuff if this were to occur; I was
    non-committal.
  2. On the issue of dock lights we took out the
    approved dock plans
    which do have electrical details; since electrical
    work of one sort or
    another has already been approved, the agency would
    be satisfied if we
    simply sent a letter saying that we are modifying
    those details to the
    extent that we are putting in an emergency light
    system, probably the
    existing letter would suffice.
  3. It appears that once this is done we could and
    would come in with a
    series of minor permit applications for the main
    house, library and so on.
  4. I also took the time to meet with Douglas Hodge
    to go over the
    building permit application process which we are
    initiating for the various
    buildings, which, as you know, will be on a separate
    track; he is primed and
    I want to get the office and pool documents into his
    hands asap.
  5. We did spend some time on the R/O in take issue:

a. When we were on the island yesterday, Mr. Oriel
mentioned that at
one time a new R/O intake line was requested on the
south east side of the
island;
b. Apparently that request was dropped and a line
running out or under
the dock was substituted;
c. What is the scope of the current request?
d. The current master plan does not contemplate a
new intake line; a
new well is contemplated; I would prefer figuring
out a well strategy and
getting away from intake approvals because we get
away from a lot of
bureaucracy in doing so.

  1. There is something in me that makes me nervous
    about withdrawing the
    office permit, but I have the impression that the
    people we are dealing with
    are being honest and they will complete reviews
    promptly.
  2. I am going to get some work done on the master
    plan document and I
    am going to target filing it next week; I should
    like input from you both
    about the content of this message.

Do You Yahoo!?
Tired of spam? Yahoo! Mail has the best spam protection around
http://mail.yahoo.com

subject: Re: CZM Meeting
body:
I'm ok with following theier advice,, document why we
are modifying...  r/o intake... talk to miles on
Monday,,I know nothing about it. fine on inspection

> 10.	I am going to get some work done on the master
> plan document and I

> like input from you both
> about the content of this message.

--- John Amerling jamerling@rrcaribbean.com wrote:

I SHOULD ADD A POSTCRIPT. IN MY MEETING WITH HODGE
HE WELCOMED THE PROSPECT
OF SELF INSPECTION OF CONSTRUCITON WORK. THE
PRACTICE IS MORE EXPENSIVE, BUT
IT WILL AVOID A LOT OF DELAY.

Here is a summary of what happened today in meeting
with Mr. Oriel and Ms
Peterson;

  1. I showed them portions of our study of our report
    concerning
    existing conditions and future plans.
  2. Ms Peterson suggested that:

a. we take the full buildout drawing, FB 1, and
flesh it out with
phase designations and a brief narrative and submit
it covered by a Minor
Application form;
b. the agency would accept this and then use it as a
baseline document
in connection with future reviews of all
applications

  1. They suggested that both the office and the pool
    related expansions
    be submitted as a modification to Permit

#CZT-53-04L:

a. The modification would seek approval of both the
office and the pool
expansions
b. The modification would mention infrastructure
improvements which we
want to cover, such as trenching for utilities and
placement of pads and
structures to house the R/O expansion
c. The modification would mention the request to add
water to the salt
pond on the grounds that this was a land side, not a
marine issue
d. The cover letter would say that the current minor
application for
the office is being withdrawn; they swore up and
down that the would review
the modification right away and not set the whole
process back.

  1. Mr. Oriel continued to advocate using the base
    permit for the dock
    as the fulcrum for dealing with any changes we
    needed to the barging areas,
    stone in the area of the urchins, floating dock and
    so on; he mentioned the
    need to ask for a waiver of time or "temporary
    removal" of these items from
    the Corps stuff if this were to occur; I was
    non-committal.
  2. On the issue of dock lights we took out the
    approved dock plans
    which do have electrical details; since electrical
    work of one sort or
    another has already been approved, the agency would
    be satisfied if we
    simply sent a letter saying that we are modifying
    those details to the
    extent that we are putting in an emergency light
    system, probably the
    existing letter would suffice.
  3. It appears that once this is done we could and
    would come in with a
    series of minor permit applications for the main
    house, library and so on.
  4. I also took the time to meet with Douglas Hodge
    to go over the
    building permit application process which we are
    initiating for the various
    buildings, which, as you know, will be on a separate
    track; he is primed and
    I want to get the office and pool documents into his
    hands asap.
  5. We did spend some time on the R/O in take issue:

a. When we were on the island yesterday, Mr. Oriel
mentioned that at
one time a new R/O intake line was requested on the
south east side of the
island;
b. Apparently that request was dropped and a line
running out or under
the dock was substituted;
c. What is the scope of the current request?
d. The current master plan does not contemplate a
new intake line; a
new well is contemplated; I would prefer figuring
out a well strategy and
getting away from intake approvals because we get
away from a lot of
bureaucracy in doing so.

  1. There is something in me that makes me nervous
    about withdrawing the
    office permit, but I have the impression that the
    people we are dealing with
    are being honest and they will complete reviews
    promptly.
  2. I am going to get some work done on the master
    plan document and I
    am going to target filing it next week; I should
    like input from you both
    about the content of this message.
